title: InstCombine: Handle rounding intrinsics in SimplifyDemandedFPClass
summary:
The generalization strategy involved extracting a reusable helper function (`KnownFPClass::roundToIntegral`) that encapsulates the common propagation logic for all rounding intrinsics (trunc, floor, ceil, rint, nearbyint, round, roundeven). This unified the previously scattered per-intrinsic handling into a single routine that computes result `KnownFPClass` based on source constraints, eliminating code duplication.

The transform then applies this helper in `SimplifyDemandedUseFPClass` to handle all these intrinsics uniformly, enabling consistent simplification patterns: eliminating redundant calls when the source is known to be infinity, NaN, or zero; folding subnormal inputs to constants (e.g., floor of negative subnormal → -1.0); and propagating `nnan`/`ninf` attributes. This approach scales to support the many test cases by parameterizing behavior via intrinsic ID and type properties, ensuring robust coverage across floating-point classes and rounding modes.
